items.content: Project name is 'taskflow-api' — a REST API backend for a task management app
Technology stack: FastAPI, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, Pydantic v2, Alembic for migrations, uv for package management
Using Pydantic v2 specifically for performance improvements; aware of breaking changes with @validator → @field_validator migration
User prefers uv for package management over pip or poetry due to speed
Project structure: app/ (with main.py, models/, schemas/, routers/, services/, db.py), alembic/, tests/, pyproject.toml. Business logic belongs in services, not route handlers.
Minimum test coverage requirement: 80%
Testing stack: pytest with pytest-asyncio for async tests and httpx for testing FastAPI endpoints
Deployment target: Docker containers deployed to Fly.io using multi-stage Dockerfile for optimization
CI/CD: GitHub Actions pipeline runs ruff linting, pytest with coverage checks, and Docker build on main branch
Using ruff as the linter/formatter (replaces flake8, isort, and others)

items.content: CI/CD pipeline: GitHub Actions with stages lint → test → build docker image → push to GHCR → deploy to Fly.io. Deployment to Fly.io only triggers on main branch.
Test matrix strategy: validate Python 3.11 and 3.12 compatibility. Both versions must be supported.
Frontend stack: Next.js 14 with App Router, TypeScript mandatory (no plain JavaScript files allowed).
Frontend design system: shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS. No Material UI or Chakra UI.
API rate limiting: tiered per-user limits — 100 requests/minute for free tier, 1000 requests/minute for paid tier.
Rate limit implementation: Redis with sliding window algorithm using ZSET. Reuses existing Redis job queue infrastructure.
Feature flag naming convention: {team}_{feature_name}_{state} where state is enabled/disabled/rollout. Example: payments_new_checkout_enabled. Enforced in LaunchDarkly.
Feature flag management platform: LaunchDarkly.

items.content: Architecture: modular monolith. Extract to microservices only when clear scaling need arises.
API: GraphQL with Apollo Server for multi-client support (web, mobile, third-party integrations).
Authentication: OAuth2 with PKCE flow for all clients. JWT tokens using RS256 algorithm. No API key authentication for first-party clients.
Search infrastructure: Elasticsearch for complex full-text search requirements.
Caching: Redis for session cache and hot data. TTL: 1 hour default, 24 hours for user profiles.
Background jobs: Celery with Redis broker. Periodic tasks via Celery Beat. No separate job service.
File storage: S3-compatible via Cloudflare R2 with Cloudflare CDN. Rationale: zero egress costs vs AWS S3.
Observability stack: Grafana Loki for structured JSON logs, Grafana Tempo for traces via OpenTelemetry. Rationale: lighter than ELK.
Infrastructure as Code: Terraform for all cloud resources. Manual console changes strictly forbidden — everything must be in IaC.
Secrets management: HashiCorp Vault for all secrets. Secrets must not be stored in environment variables directly — must go through Vault. Non-negotiable policy.
Error tracking: Sentry. Integrate into all services.
Code review policy: PRs require 2 approvals minimum. No self-merge on main branch. Main branch is protected. Feature flags required for all risky deployments.

items.content: Primary GitHub organization is github.com/acme-corp. All project repositories live under this organization.
Primary programming language across the organization is Python. TypeScript is used for frontend only.
Always run tests after making code changes without being asked. Do not skip tests.
Follow conventional commits format for all commit messages. Format: type(scope): message. Types are: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test. Always include scope in parentheses.

Staging environment is at staging.acme-corp.com with a separate database from production. Production environment is at acme-corp.com. Never mix or cross-contaminate databases between staging and production.
Prefer short, direct responses. Use bullet points only when there are 3 or more items. Do not include preamble.
On-call rotation occurs every 2 weeks. Critical alerts are posted in Slack channel #platform-alerts.
When reviewing code, prioritize in this order: security issues first, then correctness, then style.
